source: RMC-BestFit/src/RMC.BestFit/Analyses/Univariate/Bulletin17CAnalysis.cs @ fc28c0c -- A11 EXACT oracle, verified against the real RMC.BestFit Bulletin17CAnalysis by the dotnet gate. Bulletin17CAnalysis fits an LP3 model by GMM (default MultivariateNormal UQ) then computes the deterministic Cohn-style delta-method CIs (nested Gaussian quadrature over the GMM fit -- no MCMC/bootstrap seed dependence given the fitted parameters). The GMM fit uses BFGS + a numerical Jacobian (no RNG), so the parameters/point_estimates/CIs reproduce bit-identically across C++/R/Python; parameter + point_estimate match C# to rel 1e-9, the Cohn CI bounds to rel 1e-8 (the nested quadrature is a distinct summation path). confidence_level and the exceedance-probability round-trip are exact structural invariants. The 25 default exceedance ordinates ascend, so the point-estimate curve descends. X8/X9 arms: the lp3_linked_multivariate_normal and lp3_bias_corrected_bootstrap cases drive the two un-gated uncertainty methods (LinkedMultivariateNormal / BiasCorrectedBootstrap). ComputeCohnStyleConfidenceIntervals is a diagnostic delta-method computed off the RNG-free GMM point estimate ALONE (independent of UncertaintyMethod, which only governs the heavy parameter-set UQ that populates BayesianAnalysis.Results, not the Cohn curve), so those two cases assert the SAME deterministic Cohn point_estimate/CI/parameter oracle -- exercising the ParseUncertaintyMethod dispatch arms end-to-end without a chaotic seeded curve.
